title: Palak Dal with Roti
description: Spinach folded into soft dal, served with rotis—a greens-forward complete meal for lunch or dinner.
explanation: Palak dal puts leafy greens at the center of a familiar Indian plate. The dal provides body and protein; spinach supplies volume, iron-friendly foliage, and color.
Onion and garlic are part of the standard version. The plate is complete with roti so it works as a single main in a diet plan.
ingredients: 1 cup Mixed Dal (moong + masoor or toor), rinsed
3 cups Palak (spinach), washed and roughly chopped
instructions: Cook dal with water, turmeric, and salt until soft and mashable. Keep slightly thick for roti.
Heat oil, add cumin, then onion; saute until golden. Add ginger-garlic and tomato; cook until soft.
Add chilli powder, then spinach. Cook until spinach wilts and darkens, 3–4 minutes.
Stir the spinach mixture into the dal. Simmer 5 minutes so flavors combine. Adjust salt and thickness with water if needed.
Finish with lemon. Serve hot with rotis.
stepNotes: Do not overcook spinach into a dull paste—wilted and mixed is enough.
If using frozen spinach, squeeze out excess water before adding.
A thicker dal scoops better with roti; thin it only if serving with rice instead.
Wash spinach thoroughly; grit ruins the bowl.
Make the tadka in a separate pan if you prefer a cleaner process.

variationSections.title: Greens swaps
items: Use methi leaves for a more bitter, aromatic dal.
Mix spinach with a little bathua or amaranth when in season.
Add a handful of chopped dill for a different herbal note.

variationSections.title: Plate adjustments
items: Millet roti for a gluten-free variation.
Skip onion and garlic and use hing for a no-onion/no-garlic variation (not the default).
Add a side salad when you want more raw vegetables.
